Barrera And Associates Construction, Inc.S Notice Of Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1281.5
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BARRERA & ASSOCIATES CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1281.5.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to stay action pending arbitration is denied without prejudice. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.5(b), a claimant may "file and serve a motion and notice of motion pursuant to Section 1281.4 to stay the action pending the arbitration of any issue, question, or dispute that is claimed to be arbitrable under the agreement and that is relevant to the action to enforce the claim of lien." Section 1281.4 allows a court to order a stay when it has ordered arbitration or there is a pending petition to arbitrate. Even where no order or application for an order compelling arbitration has been made, "it is within the implicit power of the court to stay the legal proceedings while arbitration is proceeding." (Seidman & Seidman v. Wolfson (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 826, 834.) Here, however, no arbitration is proceeding, and plaintiff has not moved to compel arbitration. The Court declines the parties' implicit invitation to issue an advisory opinion regarding whether defendants are bound by the Home Improvement Contract to arbitrate the dispute. This ruling is without prejudice to plaintiff renewing its request for a stay in conjunction with a motion to compel arbitration.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
